items.title: AMZ123亚马逊导航-跨境电商出海门户
items.excerpt: AMZ123亚马逊导航是一家致力于服务中国跨境电商从业者的综合平台，以让跨境电商出海更便捷为使命，始终围绕卖家需求，为卖家提供实时的跨境资讯，实用的跨境干货、工具、数据和服务，打造一站式跨境流量入口。做跨境电商，就上AMZ123。

items.title: A guide to targeting with Sponsored Products
items.excerpt: Discover tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products.
items.body: Guide A simple guide to effective targeting with Sponsored Products Tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products. Table of contents 1 . An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting What is targeting? 2 . Targeting options with Sponsored Products Automatic targeting Manual targeting Negative targeting Deciding which targeting strategy 3 . Automatic targeting with Sponsored Products Remember this When to choose automatic targeting Just getting started with Sponsored Products? Already have active manual campaigns? 4 . Manual targeting (keywords) with Sponsored Products More about match types Remember this Top tips Keyword types Do your research To note, for Kindle Direct Publishing (KDP) authors and book vendors Choose keyword targeting when: 5 . Manual targeting (product) with Sponsored Products Remember this Choose product targeting when: 6 . Negative targeting with Sponsored Products Consider adding the following as negative keywords Remember this 7 . Setting up strategic targeting for Sponsored Products campaigns How to be strategic Start by defining your business goals Next, choose your campaign strategy 6 tips to remember How campaign structure can impact advertising performance 8 . Sponsored Products targeting checklist An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting Strong targeting is crucial to build a solid foundation for your Sponsored Products campaigns, and can help to match shoppers intents to your products. What is targeting? Targeting is the way you define the context in which you want your ads to appear. That can be done in multiple ways. The most common is the use of keywords that match your ad with the shopping queries customers use when looking for something online. This guide will explore everything you need to know about...

items.title: Fix Brand Name Errors After Brand Registry Without Starting Over
items.excerpt: Amazon brand name changes can be fixed when a listing is marked generic after Brand Registry. Sellers need product photos, packaging photos, brand name proof, UPC details, and clear evidence. Generic ASIN issues can be corrected when Amazon support receives the right brand verification proof. Do not let Amazon keep your branded product stuck as generic; get help before listing errors cost ranking, trust, and sales:...
items.body: Amazon brand name changes can be fixed when a listing is marked generic after Brand Registry. Sellers need product photos, packaging photos, brand name proof, UPC details, and clear evidence. Generic ASIN issues can be corrected when Amazon support receives the right brand verification proof. items.title: AI + Google Trends实战：如何从热搜里找到产品机会？
items.excerpt: 这一篇，我想聊一个很多跨境卖家都在看，但用的不够深的工具：Google Trends 我们平时关注热搜词和趋势，但热搜词本身只是信号，不是机会/需求本身。 每一个搜索词背后，都是潜在的需求和趋势变化： 会不会变成 Amazon 上的产品？ 会不会变成配件需求？ 会不会变成内容流量？ 会不会带来新的站内搜索词变化？ 或者只是一次短期情绪波动？ 热搜词本身不是机会，我们需要对热搜词做进一步的分析和长期的判断。 进入正题，今天就结合AI，分...

items.title: 亚马逊自然流量到底怎么形成和落地？怎么让它自己长出来？
items.excerpt: 上篇扒了一个收费19800的「北大客座教授」，顺手把他课程里的CPC优化逻辑免费讲了（没看的建议先翻上篇）。 今天拆另一半——自然流量到底怎么形成的，怎么落地。 好了，该交一半的钱了（笑） 好了，直接进入正题。 很多运营对自然流量的理解就一句话：「多出单，排名就上去了。」 这句话不算错，但跟「多赚钱，日子就好了」差不多——对是对，屁用没有。 你得知道亚马逊凭什么给你自然流量，不然你就永远在烧广告换单，自然位纹丝不动。 先给一个核心框架...

items.title: 科学选品逻辑胜于“运气”，为什么都是看数据选品, 你却很难找到爆款? 真正的破局, 不能靠卷, 而是要会算
items.excerpt: 为什么都是看数据选品, 你却很难找到爆款? 选品看数据, 却始终看不到背后本质, 广告一直投, 算账却始终不见利润。新品难推、老品难守, 运营动作越多, 风险反而越高。 为什么大卖能持续霸榜, 中小卖却做不上去? 差距不一定是不努力, 而是努力的方向真的对吗? 头部卖家用选品-广告-供应链协同形成正循环, 中小卖零散操作、被动应对, 只能在红海里反复试错。 为什么始终找不到属于自己的可持续盈利模型? 看不清隐形盈利结构、广告回报、沉淀...

items.title: 关于5月18日以后新规价格机制变化的探讨和疑问
items.excerpt: 5.18亚马逊新规价格即将落地，从改版后的规则来看，我理解的参考价的计算逻辑变化： 1、参考价来源确定为：经验证的建议零售价 or 按90天内成交价中位数计算过后的价格 2、价格分类的确定： 促销价格：deal价格、coupon价格、promotion价格 常规价格：ped价格（5.18号以后）、原价 以US站为例，参考价计算方法： 促销价格的天数不超过50%，参考价=常规价格的中位数 促销价格的天数超过50%，参考价=（常规价格+促...

items.title: #747 - 3 Sellers Share What’s Working Now on Amazon, Walmart, and TikTok Shop
items.excerpt: Three real sellers share how they found winning products, scaled across Amazon, Walmart, and TikTok Shop, used AI, and grew brands from early traction to millions in sales.
items.body: Three real sellers share how they found winning products, scaled across Amazon, Walmart, and TikTok Shop, used AI, and grew brands from early traction to millions in sales. Get Helium 10 with a special discount to start or scale your e-commerce business here: https://h10.me/h10 ► Watch The Podcasts On Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1 ► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To Save 10% For Life) ► Learn How To Sell on Amazon: https://h10.me/ft In this episode of the Serious Sellers Podcast, Bradley Sutton sits down with three entrepreneurs at the Ecomm Mastery AI event in Nashville to unpack very different paths to e-commerce growth. From a toy brand built around functional products for kids, to a niche supplement company scaling through Amazon and TikTok Shop, to a lawn and garden brand navigating Vendor Central, 3P selling, Walmart, and AI-driven advertising, this conversation covers a wide range of seller experiences. First, we hear how one founder combined an engineering background from MIT, toy industry experience, and digital marketing skills to build the kids’ toy brand Quiggly that hit $850,000 in its first full year. Her hero product, a functional toy spray mop, came from identifying long-tail keyword demand around Montessori-style toys and creating a standalone product customers were already searching for. Her biggest lesson: product-market fit matters more than launching a wide product catalog.
